methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Space Forge is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Joshua Western and Andrew Bacon that has raised $22.6M to date. The valuation universe comprises five public-market comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Only one triangulation method was applicable to this analysis. Stage-progression anchoring uses the $22.6M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage progression, implying a valuation of $45M. The peer-median approach could not be deployed due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow Space & Aerospace cohort (only two adjacent-stage peers identified, below the three-datapoint threshold for statistical reliability). Public-comp anchoring likewise could not be applied because Space Forge's revenue is not disclosed—a standard constraint for private company analysis—despite the public sector median trading at 25.8x revenue across the five identified tickers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $38M to $52M, with a median point estimate of $45M. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer benchmarking, lack of disclosed financials to anchor multiples analysis, and reliance on a single methodological input. Any forward valuation work should incorporate revenue or margin guidance if made available, and sensitivity to the stage-progression multiplier assumption (currently 2.0x) is recommended.
